description: Pathfinder, research-note, and exploratory baselines. These do not witness the base claim. Their outputs are direction notes, alternative-hypothesis suggestions, and guardrails — not theory verdicts.

hypotheses.plain_statement: Zero-reuse / zero-handling optimization (scaled-zero reconstruction at physical coordinates) behaves consistently with the baseline reconstruction under the declared tolerance.

expected_signature.pass_rule: scaled-zero reconstruction agrees with baseline within tolerance

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Zero-reuse is a research-engineering optimization. If it disagrees with the baseline reconstruction, then either the optimization is wrong or the baseline depends on a hidden detail of zero-handling.
failure_means: the zero-reuse optimization may be invalid in this window
the engineering shortcut is not safe yet
the baseline reconstruction may rely on hidden zero-handling details
possible_alternative_hypotheses: zero-reuse is valid only at low |k|
zero-reuse requires a specific normalization to match the baseline
the baseline implicitly does something the optimization skips
intended_inference_if_passed: the zero-reuse engineering optimization is consistent with the baseline on this window
it is safe to reuse zeros under the tested settings

hypotheses.plain_statement: A translation-vs-dilation comparison should indicate which transform family is more coherent under the tested reconstruction conditions.

expected_signature.expected_value: directional signal indicating one family preferred
expected_signature.pass_rule: the result decisively favors dilation over translation (or vice versa)

hypotheses.why_this_matters: Pathfinders inform which gauge direction to prioritize when allocating proof effort. A decisive PATH-1 result narrows the formalization target.
failure_means: the pathfinder is not decisive under the current metric or window
the decision margin may be too tight
the comparison metric may need revision
possible_alternative_hypotheses: the preferred direction depends on the test window
translations and dilations are both partially coherent on different sub-windows
the coherence metric is dominated by precision floor
intended_inference_if_passed: one transform family is more coherent on the tested window
this informs prioritization of formalization effort
this is a direction note, not a theorem
disallowed_conclusions: this rules out the non-preferred direction in general

hypotheses.plain_statement: A scaled-zero correspondence should show meaningful nearest-neighbor behavior under the tested lattice / zero relation.

expected_signature.pass_rule: the correspondence metric exhibits structure beyond noise

hypotheses.why_this_matters: PATH-2 explores whether a particular zero-correspondence formulation has any chance of supporting an exact predicate transport down the road.
failure_means: the chosen correspondence metric is weak or wrong on this window
exact transport via this particular metric is unlikely without revision
the lattice formulation may need to be replaced
possible_alternative_hypotheses: a different distance metric reveals the correspondence
the correspondence holds only modulo a structured residue
intended_inference_if_passed: the chosen correspondence metric shows structure on the tested window
this metric remains a candidate for further formalization
this is a direction note, not a theorem

hypotheses.plain_statement: Direct pointwise transport of zeta — i.e. ζ(t) = ζ(c^k t) — is not expected to be invariant under arbitrary multiplicative scaling. Residuals should quantify and bound this guardrail.

expected_signature.pass_rule: the residual is bounded and structured, consistent with non-trivial guardrail behavior

hypotheses.why_this_matters: TRANS-1 protects against the misreading that the gauge claim requires literal pointwise zeta equality. The base claim is about same-object behavior under the gauge, not pointwise function equality.
failure_means: the guardrail or its current interpretation needs review
the residual signal may need a different normalization or summary
the test window may need revision
possible_alternative_hypotheses: the guardrail metric should be expressed as a relative residual
pointwise transport behaves differently inside and outside the critical strip
intended_inference_if_passed: the guardrail is in place on the tested window
the gauge-coordinate theory is not being mistakenly tested as pointwise zeta equality
